documentation.readme: A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.
usage_sections: Support find more domains from HTTPS cert
Some extra code to work with Python2.7 / 3.6 / 3.7 / 3.8 / 3.10
Try to use Proactor event loop under Windows
更好的兼容性。使用 Python 2.7 / 3.6 / 3.7 / 3.8 / 3.10 测试

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `subDomainsBrute` from its upstream repository, then run `subDomainsBrute --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.

name: subDomainsBrute
summary: A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use subDomainsBrute because A fast sub domain brute tool for pentesters, works with Python3.5+ or Python2.7.
When chaining exploit tools, pair subDomainsBrute with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `subDomainsBrute --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
